findings.comment: The launcher writes the initial registry, starts the detached supervisor, then rewrites the whole stale registry object just to add supervisor_pid. If the supervisor has already read-modify-written child_pid/child_pgid/containment or a terminal status, this parent write can clobber that evidence back to status=running without child containment data, defeating the session-scoped pidfile registry and orphan-reaper evidence required by T100/SC-025. Update the post-spawn path to merge supervisor_pid into the current on-disk registry instead of rewriting the stale pre-spawn object, and cover the fast-supervisor race.

The non-Windows detached review spawn still passes Start-Process -WindowStyle Hidden. PowerShell only supports -WindowStyle on Windows; the isolated-task launcher correctly gates this parameter with $IsWindows, but this Unix/WSL branch does not. On WSL/Linux the detached service path can fail before starting the reviewer, contradicting the claimed WSL-validated Stop-hook detached spawn and cross-platform supervisor requirements. Gate -WindowStyle to Windows or remove it from the non-Windows branch and add a Unix/WSL spawn regression test.
